faqs.question: What are water conservation retrofits?
faqs.answer: Water conservation retrofits involve upgrading your existing plumbing fixtures and systems with water-efficient alternatives, such as low-flow toilets, faucet aerators, efficient showerheads, and smart irrigation controllers.

faqs.question: How much water can I save with retrofits?
faqs.answer: Most homeowners see a 20–40% reduction in water usage after retrofitting. Low-flow fixtures alone can save thousands of gallons per year, which translates to significant savings on your water bill.

faqs.question: Are there rebates available for water-saving upgrades?
faqs.answer: Yes, many local water utilities and the city of {city} offer rebates for water-efficient fixtures and appliances. We can help you identify which upgrades qualify and assist with the rebate application process.
